etymology.description: 人(人) (rén, "person") refers to individual people, while 員(員) (yuán, "member") denotes a member of a group or organization. Together, 人員(人员) (rényuán, "personnel; staff") refers to the people who are members of a workforce or staff.
etymology.interpretation: 人(人) (rén, "person") represents individuals, and 員(員) (yuán, "member") implies belonging to a group or organization. Thus, 人員(人员) (rényuán) signifies individuals collectively as members of an organization's staff. This combination highlights the idea of individuals functioning collectively within the framework of a workforce or team.

examples.chinese: 公司人员正在开会(公司人員正在開會)。
examples.english: The company personnel are having a meeting.

examples.chinese: 所有的医务人员都很专业(所有的醫務人員都很專業)。
examples.pinyin: Suǒyǒu de yīwù rényuán dōu hěn zhuānyè.
examples.english: All the medical staff are very professional.

examples.chinese: 新员工在接受培训(新員工在接受培訓)，以便融入团队(以便融入團隊)。
examples.pinyin: Xīn yuángōng zài jiēshòu péixùn, yǐbiàn róngrù tuánduì.
examples.english: The new employees are undergoing training to integrate into the team.
